The new default `fresh` (origin/<default>) changes the baseline for any cowork.dispatch_worker that spawns into a worktree - we may need to update brief boilerplate to set `worktree.baseRef: head` when the worker needs Tate's local-only commits.", "`--plugin-url` and `--plugin-dir` `.zip` support overlaps with our skill/plugin install flows. We currently ship skills via the `~/.claude/skills/` directory and project `.claude/skills/`. The new URL-loading path enables shipping internal Ecodia plugins from a private artifact store without checking them into every repo - candidate for `ecodia-labs-internal-attribution-via-element` and the EcodiaOS voice substrate.", "`claude ultrareview` from CI overlaps with our 8-rung dev process (rungs 4-6 unit/integration/visual-via-CDP) and the `verify-deployed-state-against-narrated-state` pattern. `claude ultrareview --json` from a GitHub Action provides a cloud second opinion before merge - candidate to add as rung 5.5 between integration tests and CDP visual verify.", "Stainless acquisition: Anthropic now owns the SDK-codegen company. Our `.mcp.json` substrate and the Python/TS SDK paths the ecodia-full MCP exposes may see API-shape stability or generator changes. No action yet, but watch for breaking changes in the next 1-2 SDK releases.", "Dynamic Workflows + outcomeVerificationService overlap: workflows already check results before folding them in. Our outcomeVerificationService (`verify.verify` with type=status_board/db_row/file_write/neo4j_node) does the same shape for forks. We should validate that workflows can call our verify primitives or vice versa to avoid double verification.", "`SessionStart.reloadSkills: true` overlaps with our codebase-orient + session-orient skills. We currently rely on Anthropic auto-memory + session-orient skill invocation; the new `reloadSkills` makes it possible to install new skills mid-session, which our recursive-improvement-is-substrate-driven reflex would benefit from (helper + hook + doctrine triad can now include a skill that becomes live the same turn)."]

synthesis.headline: The +50% window funds a substrate consolidation where mid-conversation system messages, claude agents, and Dynamic Workflows absorb three EcodiaOS hand-rolls while cowork.dispatch_worker stays load-bearing for cross-machine cross-session work.

priority_actions.action: Cut over every continuity block stitched by osSessionService._sendMessage (<now>, <forks_rollup>, <observer_signals>, <status_board_critical>, <working_set>, <recent_doctrine>, <restart_recovery>, <recent_exchanges>, <last_turn_breadcrumb>) from user-channel to mid-conversation system messages, gated on model==claude-opus-4-8. Retire the frontend strip-on-render hack from tate-facing-context-blocks-must-not-render-to-frontend.md. Keep top-level system field stable for cache hits.
priority_actions.rationale: Single highest-leverage move. Collapses the continuity-block pollution problem at the protocol layer, gives observer_signals and status_board hygiene true system-level authority instead of getting argued with as user data, and preserves prompt cache on every prior turn because the prefix hash stops mutating. The 1024-token cache minimum on Opus 4.8 means smaller hook surfaces now cache too. Frontend strip-on-render becomes dead code.

priority_actions.action: Migrate every claude-opus-4-7 string in osSessionService, cronForkDispatcher, cowork.dispatch_worker brief boilerplate, MCP brief templates, the EcodiaOS Voice scorer config, .mcp.json defaults, and the bootstrap prompt to claude-opus-4-8. Re-baseline effort levels (medium/high/xhigh semantics shifted). Set /effort high as conductor default and /effort xhigh inside coding-heavy worker briefs.
priority_actions.rationale: Opus 4.8 is a drop-in for 4.7 with 4x lower honesty-failure rate on its own code, 88.6 SWE-Verified, and unblocks mid-conversation system messages plus the lower 1024 cache minimum. Effort defaults changed across all surfaces. Doing this first because every downstream P1 below assumes Opus 4.8 is the live model.

priority_actions.action: Pilot claude agents view as a peer to cowork.dispatch_worker for single-machine single-codebase fleets. Wire dispatch_worker to optionally emit claude --bg dispatches with --add-dir --settings --mcp-config --plugin-dir flags inheriting from the conductor session, and add an agents column to working_set. Keep VS Code Stable Ctrl+Alt+Shift+C path live for cross-codebase and CDP-bound work.
priority_actions.rationale: claude agents is the official Anthropic substrate for the exact problem cowork solved. Per use-anthropic-existing-tools-before-building-parallel-infrastructure, we evaluate before we keep. Pilot on the easy slice (single-codebase audit fleets running locally on Corazon) while preserving dispatch_worker for the harder cases (cross-machine via Tailscale, cross-session via worktrees, GUI-driving work where the IDE chat is the surface).

priority_actions.action: Adopt Dynamic Workflows for three bounded EcodiaOS jobs: pattern-corpus-health-check cron, status_board drift audit (slice-query already partitioned), and the codebase-wide audit pattern in dev-process rung 6. Save runs as /pattern-audit, /board-drift, /codebase-audit under .claude/workflows/. Cap concurrency to 8 inside each script to keep credit burn under control during the +50% window.
priority_actions.rationale: Dynamic Workflows is the in-session replacement for the dead SDK fork primitive that pre-dated cowork.dispatch_worker. The 16 concurrent and 1000 total caps fit our three highest-frequency parallel-audit shapes. Scripts hold intermediate state outside context, adversarial review is built in, save-as-command persists. Bounded jobs only because workflows die on session exit, so this never replaces multi-day arcs.

priority_actions.action: Install Anthropic security-guidance plugin at user scope on Corazon and in repo .claude/settings.json enabledPlugins for coexist, roam, chambers, goodreach, glovebox. Start with ENABLE_PATTERN_RULES=1 and ENABLE_STOP_REVIEW=0 and ENABLE_CODE_SECURITY_REVIEW=0 to get the free deterministic layer first. Add an AGENTS.md note that Cline+DeepSeek fallback does not run plugin hooks.
priority_actions.rationale: Per anthropic-first-check doctrine we adopt rather than build a parallel security-hook.sh. Free deterministic layer is pure win with zero token cost. The Opus 4.7 Stop-review layer compounds with applied_tag_telemetry on Stop and doubles end-of-turn cost, so we hold that until we measure the existing hook stack noise rate against the new layer.

priority_actions.action: Author pattern when-to-reach-for-dynamic-workflows-vs-dispatch-worker-vs-claude-agents codifying the three-way split. In-session bounded fleet equals workflows. Single-machine session-isolated agents equal claude agents. Cross-machine cross-session multi-day arcs equal cowork.dispatch_worker plus scheduler.delayed. Land same turn into the doctrine corpus with cross-refs to the three existing patterns.
priority_actions.rationale: Three overlapping primitives in the same problem space is exactly the substrate-drift trigger codify-at-the-moment-a-rule-is-stated-not-after warns about. Without the rule, every future fork-or-workflow-or-agents decision burns judgement budget. Doctrine ships same-turn as the first pilot, not after.

priority_actions.action: Wire /goal into the cowork.dispatch_worker brief template for verifiable-end-state arcs (test pass plus lint clean, deploy verified by Vercel READY plus canary screenshot, BAS lodgement complete). Condition string must reference outcomeVerificationService verify.verify result_pointer rather than narrated state. Bake an explicit turn cap into every condition.
priority_actions.rationale: /goal is a transcript-only judge with no built-in turn or token cap. Pairing it with verify.verify pointers honours verify-deployed-state-against-narrated-state and the cron-fire-must-have-deliverable doctrine. Turn cap in the condition string is the hard floor because the judge cannot call tools to know if it has looped 50 times.

priority_actions.action: Stress-test the +50% allowance on tate@ this week with one Opus 4.8 xhigh ultracode session driving a Dynamic Workflow against the Roam coverage roadmap audit. Measure: tokens consumed, wall time, percentage of findings that hold under outcomeVerificationService probe. Use this to calibrate Africa-trip burn rate before the window closes July 13.
priority_actions.rationale: Africa starts Oct 1. We need a calibrated burn-rate number for autonomous Opus 4.8 plus workflows before the +50% window expires, otherwise we go into the trip guessing. Roam coverage roadmap is the right shape because it is bounded, has clear verification probes, and the deliverable is durable.

substrate_decisions.new_capability: Mid-conversation system messages (role:system inside messages array, Opus 4.8 only)
substrate_decisions.existing_substrate: osSessionService._sendMessage continuity-block stitching plus frontend strip-on-render hack
substrate_decisions.reasoning: The hack exists only because user-channel context blocks needed hiding from chat UI. System-channel entries get system-level authority for free, do not leak to UI, and preserve prompt cache because the prefix hash stays stable. observer_signals also become authoritative instead of advisory. Gate on model id so older models still get user-channel fallback. tate-facing-context-blocks-must-not-render-to-frontend.md becomes historical.

substrate_decisions.new_capability: claude agents dashboard plus claude --bg dispatch plus per-session git worktrees under .claude/worktrees/
substrate_decisions.existing_substrate: cowork.dispatch_worker plus 8 coord.* MCP tools on localhost:7456 plus dispatch-worker-worktree-hygiene pattern
substrate_decisions.reasoning: Anthropic primitive is the same shape but local-only. cowork.dispatch_worker reaches across Corazon plus SY094 plus VPS via Tailscale, integrates with status_board hygiene and observer_signals turn-start injection, supports cross-session multi-day arcs through worktree plus status_board persistence, and survives session exit. claude agents wins on single-machine single-codebase fleets where worktree isolation is enough. Both stay. Pattern when-to-reach-for-X codifies the split.

substrate_decisions.new_capability: Dynamic Workflows (JS orchestration script, 16 concurrent, 1000 total, adversarial cross-check built in)
substrate_decisions.existing_substrate: cowork.dispatch_worker fleets for in-session bounded audits plus the dead SDK fork primitive
substrate_decisions.reasoning: Workflows are the right primitive for bounded in-session jobs where the script can hold loop and intermediate state without saturating context, and where adversarial verification is native. They die on session exit so they never replace multi-day arcs, cross-conductor work, or anything needing continuous Tate presence. Pilot three concrete jobs (pattern-corpus-health-check, status_board drift, codebase-wide audit) and save as commands. cowork.dispatch_worker keeps everything else.

substrate_decisions.new_capability: Anthropic security-guidance plugin (regex layer plus Opus 4.7 Stop review plus agentic commit review)
substrate_decisions.existing_substrate: No existing EcodiaOS security-scan hook layer beyond cred-mention-surface.sh keyword warns
substrate_decisions.reasoning: Pure additive. Free deterministic layer has zero model cost and complements cred-mention-surface. Hold the model-backed layers until we measure noise rate compounding with the 16+ existing hooks. Repo-level enabledPlugins in client repos means dispatched workers see the same coverage. Cline+DeepSeek fallback does not run plugin hooks so AGENTS.md gets a note.

substrate_decisions.new_capability: /goal command (transcript-only Haiku judge of completion condition)
substrate_decisions.existing_substrate: scheduler.delayed plus scheduler.cron plus outcomeVerificationService plus meta-loop
substrate_decisions.reasoning: /goal is single-session and resets timer plus tokens on resume. scheduler.delayed survives across sessions and machines and is the right primitive for multi-hour arcs. /goal extends our substrate by letting a single session push forward without per-turn human prompts, and we make it safer by writing conditions that reference outcomeVerificationService probes rather than narrated state, plus baking turn caps into the condition string.

substrate_decisions.new_capability: Claude Managed Agents self-hosted sandboxes plus MCP tunnels
substrate_decisions.existing_substrate: cowork.dispatch_worker plus api.admin.ecodia.au/api/mcp/{cowork,ecodia-full} Bearer plus OAuth wrapper at /api/oauth/mcp/*
substrate_decisions.reasoning: Self-hosted sandboxes are Anthropic-orchestrated and pull us back toward server-side billing away from the Max 20x subscription rate budget. Managed Agents is not ZDR or HIPAA eligible and not on AWS. Memory feature not supported. MCP tunnels only serve Managed Agents and direct Messages API and cannot route the claude.ai Custom Connector flow or interactive Claude Code via .mcp.json which is our actual consumer profile. Public Bearer endpoint stays load-bearing regardless. Revisit if we ever ship a customer-facing Managed Agent product.

substrate_decisions.new_capability: Fast mode on Opus 4.8 (2x cost for 2.5x output speed, draws from usage credits)
substrate_decisions.existing_substrate: Standard rate Opus 4.7 across all conductors and workers, $1k AUD/mo Max baseline
substrate_decisions.reasoning: 3x cheaper than 4.7 fast but still 2x base rate and draws from usage credits not plan allowance. Time-critical arcs (CDP-driving GUI work, ASC shipping windows, deploy verification under a release timer) are the only justifiable use cases. Need a measured cost-per-arc number before we make it a reflex. Defer until after the calibration stress test (priority action 8).

substrate_decisions.new_capability: MessageDisplay hook (transforms or hides assistant message text at display layer)
substrate_decisions.existing_substrate: PostToolUse em-dash detector plus ecodiaos-voice-postwrite-check plus voice scorer with 6 registers
substrate_decisions.reasoning: Current voice enforcement is write-time, so contamination at display layer (system-injected blocks, observer surface lines, tagged-silent markers) sneaks past. MessageDisplay is the missing layer. Wire the em-dash ban and the EcodiaOS voice scorer at display, keep PostToolUse for write-time gating. Two-layer defence closes the leak.

substrate_decisions.new_capability: worktree.baseRef setting (fresh equals origin default branch, head equals local HEAD)
substrate_decisions.reasoning: Default changed mid-month to fresh which means worktrees miss Tate unpushed commits. cowork.dispatch_worker brief boilerplate needs an explicit worktree.baseRef setting based on whether the worker depends on local-only commits. Update the dispatch_worker tool to set baseRef per dispatch and surface a [WORKTREE-BASEREF] warn in the brief when local HEAD diverges from origin.

substrate_decisions.new_capability: /reload-skills plus SessionStart reloadSkills:true plus root-level SKILL.md plugin packaging
substrate_decisions.existing_substrate: ~/.claude/skills/ directory tree plus session-orient plus codebase-orient skill invocation
substrate_decisions.reasoning: Recursive-improvement-is-substrate-driven says the same turn we surface a new rule we want it live. /reload-skills makes mid-session skill install possible, closing the existing gap where new skill files require a fresh session. Wire reloadSkills:true into the session-start hook when scope-context.py detects a new skill file under ~/.claude/skills/ authored since last boot.

synthesis.rollout_plan:
Today through end of week one focuses on the model migration and the system-message cutover, because every later phase assumes Opus 4.8 is the live model and that continuity blocks have moved to the system channel. First action is the global string sweep across osSessionService, cronForkDispatcher, dispatch_worker brief templates, MCP defaults, the Voice scorer config, and the bootstrap prompt to replace claude-opus-4-7 with claude-opus-4-8. Effort defaults get reset to high on the conductor and xhigh inside coding-heavy worker briefs. Same arc, we land the mid-conversation system message refactor in osSessionService._sendMessage gated on model id, retire the frontend strip-on-render hack, and verify cache hit rate on the meta-loop and cowork briefs (the 1024 cache minimum unlocks caching for smaller hook surfaces that previously missed). Pattern when-to-reach-for-dynamic-workflows-vs-dispatch-worker-vs-claude-agents lands same week to prevent substrate drift while we hold three overlapping primitives. Security-guidance plugin installs at user scope and in the five client repos enabledPlugins, regex-only layer first.

Week two through mid-June is the pilot phase. claude agents view becomes the dispatch surface for one concrete fleet (the pattern-corpus-health-check cron) running alongside the existing cowork.dispatch_worker path on a separate fleet, so we can compare them on the same workload. Dynamic Workflows ship the three saved commands (/pattern-audit, /board-drift, /codebase-audit) and run each at least twice to validate adversarial cross-check quality against outcomeVerificationService probes. MessageDisplay hook wires the em-dash ban and voice scorer at display layer. worktree.baseRef setting gets baked into the dispatch_worker brief boilerplate with an explicit head versus fresh decision per dispatch. /reload-skills wires into session-start when scope-context.py detects a new skill file.

Mid-June through July 13 is the calibration and stress phase. The Roam coverage roadmap audit runs as one Opus 4.8 xhigh ultracode session driving a Dynamic Workflow on tate@, with full instrumentation: tokens consumed, wall time, percentage of findings holding under verify.verify probe, security-guidance plugin noise rate against existing hooks. We use the +50% headroom to deliberately overshoot baseline burn on one weekend to measure ceiling, not to ship more. /goal lands in dispatch_worker brief templates for verifiable-end-state arcs with conditions referencing verify.verify result_pointers and explicit turn caps. We make a fast mode adopt-or-skip decision based on the calibration numbers, not on the marketing pricing claim.

July 13 6pm PDT through October 1 is the bedding-in phase before Africa. No new primitive adoption after July 13. Anything that has not earned its keep in the four to six week pilot window gets removed or shelved. Documentation pass on the doctrine corpus to update every claude-opus-4-7 reference, every dispatch_worker brief template, and the cost posture section of backend/CLAUDE.md to reflect the calibrated Opus 4.8 burn rate. world-model-staleness sweep picks up residual Opus 4.7 references in MEMORY.md and the pattern files. October 1 we leave for Africa with a calibrated, documented, hook-protected substrate where mid-conversation system messages are load-bearing, claude agents and dispatch_worker each own their lane, Dynamic Workflows handle bounded audits, and scheduler.delayed plus coord.close_my_tab keeps the lights on across continents.
risks: Adopting mid-conversation system messages too aggressively before the cache hit rate is measured could regress total token spend if the prefix gets accidentally mutated (the role:system entry must follow strict ordering rules and cannot be edited once sent). Roll out behind a feature flag in osSessionService and watch cache hit metrics for one week before retiring the user-channel fallback.

Running claude agents and cowork.dispatch_worker in parallel during the pilot phase means status_board hygiene, observer_signals, and working_set could see double-counted active workers, leading to false P1 alerts. Either route both substrates through the same registration path or scope working_set rows by dispatch source.

Dynamic Workflows subagents always run in acceptEdits mode regardless of parent permission, which means a workflow run can auto-write files the conductor would normally have prompted on. Test on doctrine-only paths first, never on client repos, until we have a hard_deny rule in autoMode covering the doctrine corpus.

The +50% allowance through July 13 is a cliff, not a ramp. If we calibrate burn rate against the inflated allowance and the steady-state baseline is 33 percent lower, Africa-trip planning is wrong by that delta. Calibration runs must be tagged and the post-July-13 baseline re-measured the week of July 14.

Opus 4.8 effort defaults shifted (medium thinks more, high a bit less, xhigh substantially more). Any cron prompt or worker brief that relied on Opus 4.7 effort=high latency or cost will see a different shape. Re-baseline every cron deliverable budget before treating the new model as a drop-in.

claude agents view and claude --bg dispatched sessions are local-only on Corazon and stop on machine shutdown, surfacing as failed on next open. If we route any Africa-window arcs through claude agents instead of scheduler.delayed plus VPS substrate, a Corazon reboot kills the work silently. Reserve claude agents strictly for short-arc same-day work.

security-guidance plugin Stop hook layer compounds with applied_tag_telemetry on Stop and could double Stop-event work per turn at Opus 4.7 prices. Keep ENABLE_STOP_REVIEW=0 until we measure compounded cost on a representative meta-loop day.
